---
title: "Presentation Title"
subtitle: "A short descriptive subtitle"
author: "Author Name, Role"
date: today
format: probity-beamer
lang: en-GB
---

## Agenda

- Key finding and executive summary
- Analysis and supporting evidence
- Scenario comparison
- Recommendations

## {.plain}

```{=latex}
\ProbSectionContent{Part One}{Key Finding}
```

## Revenue Exceeds the Baseline Forecast by 18%

Lead with the answer. The financial services recovery in Q2 drove outperformance
across all three modelled scenarios.

- **Revenue:** R 14.9M vs R 12.6M baseline forecast
- **Variance:** +R 2.3M (+18.3% above baseline)
- **Driver:** Q2 recovery in financial services; lag-1 GDP effect materialised
  in Q3 as anticipated

The result holds across the conservative scenario but does not extend to the
stress scenario.

## Contributing Factors

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="48%"}
**Positive drivers**

- Financial services sector recovery
- Lag-1 GDP effect, as modelled
- Lower-than-expected client attrition
- Favourable exchange rate in Q2
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
**Risk factors to monitor**

- Elevated exposure to three anchor clients
- Regulatory change in Q4 pipeline
- Margin pressure from cost inflation
- Portfolio concentration in one sector
:::

::::

## {.plain}

```{=latex}
\ProbSectionContent{Part Two}{Scenario Analysis}
```

## Scenario Results

All three scenarios outperform the prior-year actuals. The optimistic scenario
requires the Q4 pipeline to close at a conversion rate above the trailing
twelve-month average.

| Scenario      | Assumption                  | Revenue (R M) |
|:--------------|:----------------------------|---------------:|
| Baseline      | Current trend continues     |          14.9 |
| Conservative  | Q4 pipeline at 60% close    |          12.4 |
| Optimistic    | Q4 pipeline at 90% close    |          17.1 |
| Prior year    | Actual FY2025               |          11.8 |

: Provision under each scenario {#tbl-scenarios}

## Model Specification

The forecast uses an autoregressive model with a one-period GDP lag. The
specification was selected via AIC on a rolling 24-month training window.

```python
import statsmodels.api as sm

model = sm.tsa.ARIMA(
    endog=revenue,
    exog=gdp_lag1,
    order=(1, 0, 0),
)
result = model.fit()
print(result.summary())
```

## {.plain}

```{=latex}
\ProbSectionContent{Part Three}{Recommendations}
```

## Recommendations

Act on the three items below before the end of Q4 to lock in the optimistic
scenario outcome.

1. **Pursue the top-5 pipeline accounts** before the regulatory window closes
   in November. Target conversion: 85%.

2. **Reduce anchor-client concentration** to below 40% of portfolio revenue
   within two quarters. Begin cross-sell outreach immediately.

3. **Re-run the model monthly** through Q1 2027 as the GDP series revises.
   Flag any revision exceeding 1.5% to the steering committee.

## Closing

\vspace{1.2cm}

\begin{center}
{\large\color{probitynavy}\textbf{Thank you}}

\vspace{0.4cm}

{\normalsize\color{probitymuted}Questions and discussion}

\vspace{1.4cm}

{\small\color{probitymuted}Probity Data Analytics\quad\textbar\quad Confidential}
\end{center}
